user_outcome: sworn always EMITS and VALIDATES the canonical baton object form for a board's `release` (strict), while its reader still tolerates a legacy bare string — so the schema no longer carries a string-tolerance divergence from canonical baton, and any existing string board converts to canonical the next time sworn writes it.
rationale: S04 unblocked reading real coach boards by making BoardRecord.Release accept both string and object AND relaxing the embedded board-v1 schema + validator to a oneOf [string, object]. That schema/validator relaxation is permanent vendor drift from canonical baton (object-only), so every future `sworn baton vendor` re-pull of board-v1 would conflict on that field forever (the #38 class). S05 makes emit/validate canonical object-only (schema + validator + writer). AMENDED (Coach-ratified 2026-07-01): the first cut kept a lenient reader (Postel); the Coach reversed that — there is no wild data (all string boards are operator-owned), so the lenient reader has no beneficiary and a strict reader is more fail-closed (a stray string board fails loud and gets fixed instead of lurking). So the READER goes object-only too, and the operator's string boards (op-readiness/release-hygiene/conformance) are MIGRATED at cutover (AC-06) rather than self-healed-on-write. The writer still emits the object form, so nothing new is ever written as a string. Safe to tighten: `baton.Validate("board-v1")` is called only in WriteBoard on freshly-marshaled (now always object) output, and ValidateSchema is not wired for boards, so nothing enforces the schema against on-disk string boards. Builds on S04 (verified, immutable) as a separate slice per Rule 7. Out of scope: full canonical board-v1 parity (nested release.worktree etc.) — that is the broader baton re-vendor.

effort_complexity.rationale: Three small edits on S04's verified base: MarshalJSON emits object, schema release object-only, validator object-only; plus a test update. Reader unchanged.

acceptance_criteria.text: Release.MarshalJSON SHALL emit the canonical object form {"name": ...} for a name-only release (raw absent), so sworn never writes the legacy bare-string release form. A release read from a canonical object SHALL still re-emit its full object verbatim (raw preserved).

When a board.json carrying a legacy string `release` is read and then written, the written form SHALL be the canonical object {"name": <the string>} — i.e. the string board self-heals to canonical on write. Proven by TestRelease_StringReadEmitsCanonicalObject.

The embedded board-v1 schema `release` SHALL be object-only (type object, required name) — no string alternative — matching canonical baton's object form (additionalProperties:true to accept the canonical extras without enumerating them); the legacy validator SHALL likewise require the object form. AMENDED (Coach-ratified 2026-07-01, no-wild-data): the READER (Release.UnmarshalJSON) SHALL ALSO be object-only (strict) — a bare string release SHALL fail closed. No wild string boards exist; a string board is a non-migrated operator artefact and SHOULD fail loud, not be silently tolerated. Legacy operator string boards are migrated, not read-tolerated — see AC-06.

When `sworn board --release 2026-06-28-yearSnapshot-schema-cleanup` is run against the live fired repo with the S05 binary, it SHALL read the real coach OBJECT-form board (canonical — the strict reader accepts it; coach boards were always object). Reachability artefact: the board output.

After the change, `go build ./...` SHALL succeed and `go test ./...` SHALL pass (full suite — no WriteBoard/migrate test regresses on the canonical-emit change). The string-read tests SHALL be inverted to assert a bare string release now fails closed.

If the strict-reader binary is run against an unmigrated string-form board.json, it fails closed — so the operator-owned string boards (`release: "X"` -> `{"name": "X"}`) SHALL be migrated as a one-time CUTOVER step, applied ONLY once every active session is on a canonical (S04/S05) binary: a strict-reader binary fails on a string board and a pre-S04 binary fails on an object board, so the migration MUST NOT run mid-flight. This is a tracked cutover step, NOT sworn production code in this slice. NB: hold this for the operational-readiness cutover — the op-readiness board itself is among the boards to migrate.
